commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: Needs maintainer handling because the PR is protected by the maintainer label and lacks after-fix real behavior proof; there is no narrow code defect for ClawSweeper to repair.; Cleared: The diff reduces a predictable-temp-script overwrite risk and does not add dependencies, network calls, secrets handling, or broader permissions. (sha=79b96b1d2fa88f5c931e6d8689eb80254b4630fd)
commands.review_summary:
The PR moves update restart handoff scripts into generated temp subdirectories, writes them with exclusive creation, cleans up those directories, and adds focused regression coverage.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main derives the script path from `os.tmpdir()` plus a timestamp and writes without an exclusive flag; a focused unit repro can precreate or symlink that candidate path with `Date.now` mocked, though I did not run it because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: Needs maintainer handling because the PR is protected by the `maintainer` label and lacks after-fix real behavior proof; there is no narrow code defect for ClawSweeper to repair.

target.title: harden update restart script creation [AI]
target.body:
AI-assisted.

## Summary

- Problem: update restart handoff scripts used a predictable shared-temp path and default write behavior.
- Solution: create a generated temp subdirectory for each handoff script and write the script with exclusive creation.
- What changed: POSIX and Windows restart scripts now also remove their generated temp directory on best-effort cleanup.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): restart command logic, service names, task names, logging destinations, and update flow behavior are unchanged.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related: private maintainer context
- [x] This PR addresses a bug or regression

## Motivation

- The restart helper prepares a detached script during update handoff. That script should be created in an isolated temp location and should not overwrite or follow a preexisting shared-temp path.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: restart handoff script creation now avoids the old predictable shared-temp candidate path and uses exclusive creation in a generated temp subdirectory.
- Real environment tested: Not run in this handoff.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Not run in this handoff.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Regression coverage was added in `src/cli/update-cli/restart-helper.test.ts`; execution is pending maintainer verification.
- Observed result after fix: Pending maintainer verification.
- What was not tested: Real detached update restart execution and local test execution.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): N/A

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: the helper derived the script path directly from `os.tmpdir()` plus a timestamped filename, then wrote with default write semantics.
- Missing detection / guardrail: there was no regression test keeping a preexisting shared-temp candidate path untouched.
- Contributing context (if known): the restart script needs to survive the parent process and is intentionally written outside the installed package files.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/cli/update-cli/restart-helper.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: a preexisting old shared-temp candidate path remains untouched, and the generated script lives under a fresh temp subdirectory with exclusive file creation.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the behavior is contained in `prepareRestartScript` and can be validated without running a real update or service restart.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): N/A
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

None expected.

## Diagram (if applicable)

N/A

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: the generated restart script now includes best-effort temp directory cleanup in addition to existing self-deletion. No new privileged command path is introduced.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Not run in this handoff
- Runtime/container: Not run in this handoff
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A
- Relevant config (redacted): N/A

### Steps

1. Run the focused restart helper test file.
2. Confirm the regression case leaves the old shared-temp candidate untouched.
3. Confirm generated restart scripts are written in a generated temp subdirectory with exclusive creation.

### Expected

- Focused tests pass.
- Restart script path is under a generated temp subdirectory, not directly under the shared temp root.
- Existing candidate path contents are unchanged.

### Actual

- Pending maintainer verification.

## Evidence

- [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: Static review of the changed helper and regression test logic.
- Edge cases checked: unsupported platforms still return `null`; invalid Windows task names still return `null`; write failure still returns `null` after best-effort temp directory cleanup.
- What you did **not** verify: local test execution, real update flow, and detached service restart behavior.

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: temp directory cleanup may fail if the directory is no longer empty or already removed.
  - Mitigation: cleanup is best-effort and does not affect restart exit status.
target.author: pgondhi987
